text:
REGIME — Broad relief rally, growth rotation led, defensives mixed.

INDICES — VIX 24.5, falling; VIXY -2.45%. QQQ +0.60%, IJR +0.54%, SPY +0.47%, IWM +0.43%, DIA +0.37% best to worst. Small caps outpaced large caps. WTI oil (USO) was the standout cross-asset move, gaining +0.74% despite Iran de-escalation narrative dominating headlines.

SECTORS — XLP +0.94%, XLY +0.82%, XLE +0.73%, XLF +0.71%, XLK +0.58%, XLI +0.51%, XLRE +0.36%, XLC +0.05%, XLV -0.36%, XLU -0.37%, XLB -0.38%. Top industries: MSOS +6.23%, IBIT +4.08%. Bottom industries: TAN -1.26%, LIT -1.42%.

THE 7s — Defense +1.25%, Fab +1.22%, Housing +1.08%, Toll Road +1.00%, Semi +0.84%, Bank +0.80%, Industrial +0.66%, Energy +0.57%, New Staples +0.51%, Old Staples +0.45%, Insurance +0.44%, Freight +0.35%, Cloud +0.19%, Old Metals +0.05%, Medtech -0.16%, Power -0.27%, Health -0.39%, Buildout -0.40%, Software -0.63%, New Metals -0.85%. Strongest ticker in Defense 7 not specified; New Metals 7 bottom basket weakest ticker not individually reported.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 54.3, Neutral. Top movers: MSOS +6.23% (Industries), IBIT +4.08% (Industries), WGMI +2.63% (Industries). 20d momentum best sector: XLE +6.19%; worst: XLC -4.55%. Best basket 20d: Energy +8.57%; worst: Software -10.72%. Iran de-escalation drove tech and risk assets higher while compressing prior energy and utilities safe-haven premiums.

WATCH — EATZ +2.02%, 10.5x average volume; unusual vol spike warrants confirmation of follow-through. IJK +0.28%, 8.0x vol spike on minimal price move; divergence between volume and price suggests institutional positioning shift worth monitoring. XLE +0.73% today but 5d -4.60% with USO +0.74%; sector-commodity divergence unresolved heading into earnings.
